commands.description: Adds a token or memecoin to the current group's watchlist.
examples: Admin: /addtoken PEPE x=pepecoineth → Bot: Tracking PEPE.
commands.notes: Admin only. Optional x= adds official X handles for announcement monitoring.

commands.description: Turns immediate major announcement alerts on or off.
examples: Admin: /alerts on → Bot: Immediate major announcement alerts are on.

commands.description: Shows recent detected official X announcements for a token.
examples: User: /announcements PEPE → Bot: Recent PEPE announcements...
commands.notes: Requires official handles configured with /addtoken and CookMyBots X Proxy env vars.

commands.description: Generates an on-demand daily-style token digest.
examples: User: /digest PEPE → Bot: Building the digest now...
commands.notes: Uses ChainGPT through CookMyBots AI Gateway when configured.

examples: User: /help → Bot: MemePulse Watcher commands...

aiMessages.content: Build a bot that share daily insights about a particular Memecoin or token e.g it shares daily trends(up and down trends) about the token and if possible major announcement on x it share to telegram group subscribers

Built MemePulse Watcher as a complete Telegram bot with group admin setup, token tracking, CoinGecko market data, scheduled digests, optional ChainGPT summaries, CookMyBots X Proxy announcement monitoring, MongoDB persistence with in-memory fallback, docs, and deployment-safe polling.

Files touched (27): .env.sample, DOCS.md, README.md, eslint.config.js, package.json, src/bot.js…
Validation: ✅ project passes syntax/basic checks after this edit.

planMarkdown:
Bot: MemePulse Watcher

Summary:
A Telegram group bot that monitors selected memecoins or tokens, publishes daily trend summaries, and forwards important announcements from official X accounts to subscribed groups.

Product goals:
Provide Telegram communities with concise, scheduled daily insights for selected memecoins or tokens. The bot should track price movement, volume, market-cap changes, sentiment-relevant trend signals, and notable official announcements from X. Group admins can subscribe a group to one or more tokens, configure delivery time, and choose whether announcement alerts are posted immediately or included only in the daily digest. The product should prioritize reliability, clear summaries, and anti-spam controls over speculative trading advice.

High-level architecture:
Run as a single Node.js ESM service using grammY. The process initializes the bot with TELEGRAMBOTTOKEN, connects to MongoDB, loads active subscriptions, and starts polling or webhook handling depending on deployment configuration. Core modules should include: Telegram interface layer for commands and group interactions; subscription manager for group-token configuration; token market data provider abstraction for prices, volume, liquidity, market cap, and historical candles; X announcement provider abstraction for monitoring official project accounts and keywords; insight engine for comparing current metrics against previous snapshots; scheduler for daily digest jobs and periodic announcement checks; notification dispatcher for formatted Telegram messages; and persistence layer using MongoDB. The scheduler runs in-process because the service is a single process. It should maintain job state in MongoDB to avoid duplicate sends after restarts. External APIs should be wrapped behind provider interfaces so data sources can be replaced without changing bot logic. Announcement detection should prefer official X API access where available; if unavailable, use a compliant approved data provider rather than fragile scraping. All outgoing group messages should go through a rate-limited dispatcher.

Data model:
MongoDB collections: users for Telegram user profile, role hints, and first-seen timestamps; groups for Telegram chat ID, title, admin configuration status, timezone, default digest time, language, and enabled flags; tokens for symbol, name, chain, contract address, market-data provider IDs, official website, official X handles, tracking keywords, and verification status; subscriptions for group-to-token mapping, alert preferences, digest schedule, thresholds, enabled metrics, created-by admin, and status; marketsnapshots for token metrics by timestamp including price, 24h change, volume, market cap, liquidity, holder count if available, and source metadata; xposts for tracked announcements with post ID, handle, text, URL, timestamp, classification, and delivered status; digestruns for scheduled job execution state, target group, token, period, generated summary, send status, and deduplication key; auditlogs for admin actions, configuration changes, API errors, and delivery failures. Indexes should cover chat ID, token identity, subscription status, snapshot timestamps, X post IDs, and digest deduplication keys.

Key flows:
Onboarding flow: an admin adds the bot to a group, grants needed permissions, runs /setup, selects a token by symbol or contract address, confirms official X handles, sets timezone and daily digest time, then activates the subscription. Daily insight flow: scheduler finds due subscriptions, fetches latest market data, compares against previous 24h and 7d snapshots, generates a concise trend report, stores a digestrun, and sends the message to the group. Announcement flow: periodic checker fetches new posts from configured official X handles, filters for major announcement signals such as listings, partnerships, launches, burns, audits, governance, tokenomics, exploits, or emergency notices, deduplicates by post ID, then either posts immediately or queues for the next digest based on subscription settings. Manual query flow: users run token commands such as /trend or /price in the group, the bot fetches current data, applies rate limits, and replies with a compact status card. Admin management flow: admins can add or remove tokens, change alert thresholds, pause notifications, and preview the next digest. Failure flow: if external APIs fail, the bot records the failure, retries with backoff, sends degraded summaries using cached data when safe, and avoids posting stale data without labeling it.

Tech stack:
Node.js ESM runtime with grammY for Telegram bot handling. MongoDB for persistence, job state, subscriptions, snapshots, and deduplication. In-process scheduler for daily digests and periodic X checks. Market data provider integrations through HTTP APIs with provider adapters. X announcement integration through official API access or an approved data provider. Environment variables for TELEGRAMBOTTOKEN, MongoDB connection string, market data API credentials, X/API credentials, bot environment, webhook URL if used, and scheduler settings. Logging should be structured JSON. File/image output, if later needed for charts, should use generic object storage or filesystem storage.

Non-functional requirements:
Reliability: persist job state and use deduplication keys so restarts do not create duplicate messages. Scalability: single-process design should batch API calls, cache provider responses, and rate-limit outgoing Telegram messages; if usage grows, optimize within the same service before introducing new infrastructure. Security: only group admins can change subscriptions; store secrets only in environment variables; validate contract addresses and external URLs; avoid accepting arbitrary executable content. Compliance: respect Telegram rules, X access terms, and market data provider rate limits. Accuracy: label all generated insights as informational, not financial advice; show data source timestamps; distinguish confirmed official announcements from keyword-based signals. Observability: log command usage, scheduled sends, provider latency, API failures, and Telegram delivery errors. Maintainability: use clean provider interfaces, separate command handlers from business logic, and keep formatting templates centralized. Anti-spam: enforce per-group command cooldowns, announcement deduplication, alert severity thresholds, and configurable quiet hours.

Bot commands:
• start — Introduces the bot and explains how to configure token insights in a group. (usage: /start) [general]
• setup — Starts the admin setup flow for a group subscription. (usage: /setup) [admin]
• addtoken — Adds a token or memecoin to the current group's watchlist by symbol or contract address. (usage: /addtoken PEPE or /addtoken 0x...) [admin]
• removetoken — Removes a token from the current group's watchlist. (usage: /removetoken PEPE) [admin]
• tokens — Lists all tokens currently tracked in the group. (usage: /tokens) [group]
• trend — Shows the latest trend summary for a tracked token. (usage: /trend PEPE) [insights]
• price — Shows current price, 24h movement, volume, and data timestamp for a token. (usage: /price PEPE) [insights]
• digest — Generates an on-demand daily-style digest for a tracked token. (usage: /digest PEPE) [insights]
• announcements — Shows recent official X announcements detected for a tracked token. (usage: /announcements PEPE) [insights]
• settime — Sets the daily digest delivery time for the group. (usage: /settime 09:00) [admin]
• settimezone — Sets the timezone used for scheduled digests. (usage: /settimezone UTC or /settimezone America/NewYork) [admin]
• alerts — Configures immediate announcement alerts and trend threshold alerts. (usage: /alerts on or /alerts off) [admin]
• pause — Temporarily pauses scheduled digests and alerts for the group. (usage: /pause) [admin]
• resume — Resumes scheduled digests and alerts for the group. (usage: /resume) [admin]
• settings — Displays the group's current bot configuration. (usage: /settings) [admin]
• help — Shows available commands and usage examples. (usage: /help) [general]

envVarDocs.title: Telegram Bot Token
envVarDocs.description: Token used by grammY to connect to Telegram.
envVarDocs.usage: Required at boot. If missing, the bot prints guidance and exits safely.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Create a bot with BotFather on Telegram.

envVarDocs.title: MongoDB URI
envVarDocs.description: Stores groups, tokens, subscriptions, snapshots, X posts, digest runs, audit logs, cursors, and memory.
envVarDocs.usage: If missing, the bot runs with temporary in-memory storage.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Use MongoDB Atlas or another MongoDB provider.

envVarDocs.title: CookMyBots AI Endpoint
envVarDocs.description: Base URL for CookMyBots AI Gateway.
envVarDocs.usage: Used for ChainGPT Web3 digest summaries. The bot appends /chaingpt/chat internally.

envVarDocs.title: CookMyBots AI Key
envVarDocs.description: Secret key for CookMyBots AI Gateway.
envVarDocs.usage: Enables AI-generated Web3 summaries. If missing, deterministic summaries are used.

envVarDocs.title: Web3 Chat Mode
envVarDocs.usage: Defaults to on for this crypto bot.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment if you need to override behavior.

envVarDocs.title: AI Timeout
envVarDocs.description: Maximum AI request time in milliseconds.
envVarDocs.usage: Defaults to 600000 for long media or reasoning calls.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: AI Max Retries
envVarDocs.description: Retry count for transient AI gateway failures.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: Concurrency
envVarDocs.description: General concurrency control setting reserved for future internal tuning.
envVarDocs.usage: Defaults to 20. Telegram runner remains conservative for reliability.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: CookMyBots X Proxy Endpoint
envVarDocs.description: Base endpoint for CookMyBots X Proxy.
envVarDocs.usage: Used to fetch official X posts. The bot never calls X APIs directly.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Provided by CookMyBots after enabling X integration.

envVarDocs.title: CookMyBots X Proxy Key
envVarDocs.description: Secret key for CookMyBots X Proxy.
envVarDocs.usage: Required for X announcement monitoring. Users must connect X in the CookMyBots UI.

envVarDocs.title: CoinGecko API Base
envVarDocs.usage: Defaults to the public CoinGecko API base.
envVarDocs.howToGet: No key is needed for the default public integration.

envVarDocs.title: Scheduler Poll Interval
envVarDocs.description: How often the in-process scheduler checks due digests.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: Announcement Poll Interval
envVarDocs.description: How often X announcement checks run.
envVarDocs.howToGet: Set in environment only if needed.

envVarDocs.title: Default Timezone
envVarDocs.description: Default timezone for new group digest schedules.

envVarDocs.title: Default Digest Time
envVarDocs.description: Default daily digest time for new groups.
